select_when: People, teams, or systems must absorb shocks without losing the ability to keep functioning and adapt intelligently afterward.
Setbacks are likely, time horizons are long, and recovery quality matters as much as immediate performance.
The environment is volatile enough that success depends on learning, reconfiguration, and disciplined bounce-back rather than one perfect plan.
The work needs deliberate recovery and adaptation discipline instead of reactive overexposure to overwhelm.
avoid_when: Resilience is being romanticized as enduring pain indefinitely instead of redesigning the system that keeps producing preventable stress.
Leaders are using resilience language to normalize overload, underinvestment, or chronic firefighting rather than fixing root causes.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on adaptive bounce-back, learning under stress, and redesign against preventable overload rather than on generic grit rhetoric.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor-style personal-development sprawl because the raw source is stronger on adaptive systems, recovery quality, and anti-overload discipline.
Did not add higher-order fields because this batch stays inside the frozen Wave 1 to Wave 3 contracts.
open_questions: The typed input and output labels are reviewed operational compression over a source that spans self-management, systems adaptation, and organizational learning.

select_when.note: The raw strengths section is explicit on absorbing shocks, long time horizons, volatile environments, and disciplined adaptation.

avoid_when.note: Both anti-activation conditions are directly supported by the source danger lines about romanticizing endurance and normalizing overload.

input_type.note: The source activates on shock absorption and recovery under pressure, but the compact input label is reviewed operational normalization.

output_type.note: The raw source produces adaptation and bounce-back discipline; the output label compresses that into a typed recovery artifact.

reasoning_types.note: Resilience clearly uses systems and reflective self-management reasoning, but the taxonomy labels remain reviewed normalization.
